--- title: "[EventTable]{.doc-object-name .doc-class .doc-label .doc-label-dataclass}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Per-time risk-set tabulation (optionally within strata). ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_core.py#L33-L119){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.CLASS} ```python EventTable(time, n_risk, n_event, n_censor, strata=None) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Every array is aligned row-wise. When `strata` is not `None`, rows are grouped by stratum (each stratum's times are ascending). Counts are weighted when case weights are supplied, so they may be floats. ::: ## Parameter Attributes {.doc-parameter-attributes} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [time]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Array]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : [n_risk]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Array]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : [n_event]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Array]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : [n_censor]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Array]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : [strata]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Array | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : ::: ## Examples {.doc-examples} An `EventTable` is produced by `event_table`. Build one from the bundled `lung` dataset and view it as a Polars frame with `to_frame`. ::: {#61ce8571 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) et = gw.event_table(y) et.to_frame(format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
shape: (186, 4)
timen_riskn_eventn_censor
f64f64f64f64
5.0228.01.00.0
11.0227.03.00.0
12.0224.01.00.0
13.0223.02.00.0
15.0221.01.00.0
…………
840.05.00.01.0
883.04.01.00.0
965.03.00.01.0
1010.02.00.01.0
1022.01.00.01.0
``` ::: ::: ## Methods {.doc-methods}
Name Description
to_frame() Return the tabulation as a DataFrame.
### [to_frame()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-method} ::: {.doc-subject} Return the tabulation as a DataFrame. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_core.py#L75-L119){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python to_frame(*, format=None) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Exports the event-table rows with one row per unique exit time and columns for the risk set, events, censorings, and optional strata labels. ::: #### Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [format]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Output format: `None` (default), `"pandas"`, `"polars"`, or `"pyarrow"`. When `None`, a backend is auto-detected (Polars, then Pandas, then PyArrow). ::: #### Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [pandas.DataFrame, polars.DataFrame, or pyarrow.Table]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A tidy table containing `time`, `n_risk`, `n_event`, `n_censor`, and optionally `strata`. ::: #### Raises {.doc-raises} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [ImportError]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : If the requested (or, when auto-detecting, any) DataFrame library is not installed. ::: #### Examples {.doc-examples} Build an event table from the bundled `lung` dataset and convert it to a Polars frame for inspection or downstream analysis: ::: {#87764378 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) et = gw.event_table(y) et.to_frame(format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
shape: (186, 4)
timen_riskn_eventn_censor
f64f64f64f64
5.0228.01.00.0
11.0227.03.00.0
12.0224.01.00.0
13.0223.02.00.0
15.0221.01.00.0
…………
840.05.00.01.0
883.04.01.00.0
965.03.00.01.0
1010.02.00.01.0
1022.01.00.01.0
``` ::: ::: Request a different backend with `format=`: ::: {#5052bf6a .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} et.to_frame(format="pandas") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
time n_risk n_event n_censor

3 13.0 223.0 2.0 0.0
4 15.0 221.0 1.0 0.0
... ... ... ... ...

184 1010.0 2.0 0.0 1.0
185 1022.0 1.0 0.0 1.0

186 rows × 4 columns

``` ::: :::
